Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/mnt/shared-storage-user/songhaixu/miniconda3/envs/opencompass/bin/opencompass", line 3, in <module>
    from opencompass.cli.main import main
  File "/home/songhaixu/code/opencompass/opencompass/cli/main.py", line 13, in <module>
    from opencompass.runners import SlurmRunner
  File "/home/songhaixu/code/opencompass/opencompass/runners/__init__.py", line 1, in <module>
    from .dlc import *  # noqa: F401, F403
  File "/home/songhaixu/code/opencompass/opencompass/runners/dlc.py", line 18, in <module>
    from opencompass.utils import LarkReporter, get_logger
  File "/home/songhaixu/code/opencompass/opencompass/utils/__init__.py", line 4, in <module>
    from .collect_env import *  # noqa
  File "/home/songhaixu/code/opencompass/opencompass/utils/collect_env.py", line 2, in <module>
    from mmengine.utils.dl_utils import collect_env as collect_base_env
  File "/mnt/shared-storage-user/songhaixu/miniconda3/envs/opencompass/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mmengine/utils/dl_utils/__init__.py", line 3, in <module>
    from .collect_env import collect_env
  File "/mnt/shared-storage-user/songhaixu/miniconda3/envs/opencompass/lib/python3.10/site-packages/mmengine/utils/dl_utils/collect_env.py", line 10, in <module>
    import torch
  File "/mnt/shared-storage-user/songhaixu/miniconda3/envs/opencompass/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/__init__.py", line 431, in <module>
    from torch._C import *  # noqa: F403
ImportError: /mnt/shared-storage-user/songhaixu/miniconda3/envs/opencompass/lib/python3.10/site-packages/torch/lib/libtorch_cuda.so: undefined symbol: ncclCommShrink

This error is almost always a **binary mismatch between your PyTorch build and the NCCL/CUDA libraries it is loading at runtime**. `libtorch_cuda.so` expects the NCCL symbol `ncclCommShrink`, but the NCCL library found on your system/LD path is **older (or incompatible)** and doesn’t provide it.

### 1) Confirm what NCCL is being picked up
Run inside the same conda env:

```bash
python -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__)"
python -c "import torch; import os; print(torch.__file__)"
echo $LD_LIBRARY_PATH
```

Now locate the NCCL that `libtorch_cuda.so` will load:

```bash
LIBTORCH_CUDA=$(python -c "import torch,glob,os; import pathlib; \
p=pathlib.Path(torch.__file__).parent/'lib'/'libtorch_cuda.so'; print(p)")
ldd $LIBTORCH_CUDA | grep -i nccl
```

If you see it resolving `libnccl.so` from `/usr/lib`, `/usr/local/cuda`, an HPC module path, etc. (instead of something compatible with your torch), that’s the cause.

### 2) Fastest fix: use a self-consistent PyTorch + CUDA stack
Pick **one** of the two approaches and stick to it.

#### Option A (recommended): install PyTorch CUDA wheels that bundle compatible CUDA libs
```bash
conda activate opencompass
pip uninstall -y torch torchvision torchaudio
pip cache purge
pip install --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121 torch torchvision torchaudio
```
(Choose `cu118`, `cu121`, etc. to match your driver capability; this avoids relying on system NCCL/CUDA.)

#### Option B: conda-install PyTorch and NCCL together
```bash
conda activate opencompass
conda remove -y pytorch torchvision torchaudio cudatoolkit nccl
conda install -y -c pytorch -c nvidia pytorch pytorch-cuda=12.1 nccl
```

### 3) Ensure system NCCL isn’t overriding conda/pip libs
If your cluster/module environment injects CUDA/NCCL into `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, it can override the intended one.

Try temporarily sanitizing:

```bash
unset LD_LIBRARY_PATH
python -c "import torch; print('torch ok', torch.cuda.is_available())"
```

If that works, then fix by **removing the conflicting NCCL/CUDA paths** from your shell init or module loads, or by prepending the correct torch/conda lib path:

```bash
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH="$CONDA_PREFIX/lib:$LD_LIBRARY_PATH"
```

### 4) Verify the symbol exists in the NCCL being loaded
```bash
NCCL_LIB=$(ldd $LIBTORCH_CUDA | awk '/nccl/{print $3; exit}')
nm -D $NCCL_LIB | grep ncclCommShrink
```
If nothing prints, that NCCL is too old/incompatible.

---

If you paste the output of:
- `python -c "import torch; print(torch.__version__); print(torch.version.cuda)"`
- `ldd $(python -c "import torch, pathlib; print(pathlib.Path(torch.__file__).parent/'lib'/'libtorch_cuda.so')") | grep -i nccl`
- `echo $LD_LIBRARY_PATH`

…I can tell you exactly which library is conflicting and the minimal change to fix it.
